dataset: NHANES (National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey) - real patient data background; task creates synthetic lab procedure configuration

pi_items.source: Task configuration - synthetic test name created by agent UI interaction. This is not from NHANES data but is part of the task specification.
Verified against CPT code 80053 definition. Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP) is the standard name for CPT 80053 procedure code. Source: https://www.aapc.com/codes/cpt-codes/80053

Verified against authoritative medical coding sources. CPT code 80053 is the standard code for Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP). Source: https://www.aapc.com/codes/cpt-codes/80053 and https://hellomds.com/cpt-code-80053/

CPT4 (Current Procedural Terminology 4) is the standard terminology system maintained by the American Medical Association. The format 'CPT4:80053' follows standard medical coding notation. Source: https://www.ama-assn.org/practice-management/cpt/cpt-overview-and-code-approval

Glucose is a confirmed component of the Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CPT 80053). Part of the 14 substances measured in CMP. Source: https://www.clevelandheartlab.com/tests/comprehensive-metabolic-panel/
GLU is the standard medical abbreviation for Glucose used in laboratory coding systems and EHR configurations.

Creatinine is a confirmed component of the Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CPT 80053), part of the kidney function assessment group. Source: https://www.clevelandheartlab.com/tests/comprehensive-metabolic-panel/
CREAT is the standard medical abbreviation for Creatinine used in laboratory coding systems and EHR configurations.

Sodium is a confirmed component of the Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CPT 80053), part of the electrolyte assessment group. Source: https://www.clevelandheartlab.com/tests/comprehensive-metabolic-panel/
NA is the standard chemical symbol/medical abbreviation for Sodium used in laboratory coding systems and EHR configurations.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CPT 80053) with code CPT4:80053 in a LibreHealth EHR system. The procedure includes three discrete lab result components: Glucose (GLU), Creatinine (CREAT), and Sodium (NA). All values are verified against authoritative medical coding standards. The CMP is a standard 14-component blood test panel that includes these three substances. The environment uses NHANES real patient data as background, but the task creates synthetic laboratory procedure configuration data.
